name: Vaneese Thomas
abstract: Vaneese Yseult Thomas (born August 24, 1952) is an American R&B, jazz and soul blues singer, best known for her 1987 US Billboard R&B chart hit single, "Let's Talk It Over". In addition, in more recent times, she has supplied backing vocals on a long list of other musicians' work.

concerts.label: Too Hot to Handel: The Gospel Messiah
concerts.venue: Isaac Stern Auditorium / Ronald O. Perelman Stage

concerts.label: The New York Pops 35th Birthday Gala: Part of His World: The Songs of Alan Menken
concerts.venue: Isaac Stern Auditorium / Ronald O. Perelman Stage

about_quotes.speaker: David Finck
about_quotes.evidence: I did an entire record with Gladys Knight without Gladys Knight, with a really wonderful singer who tracked for her named Vaneese Thomas.
about_quotes.doc_title: David Finck interviewed by Monk Rowe, Utica, New York, May 29, 2015
